programme.desc: A man who spent more than £7,000 on an ex-police van beset with faults seeks redress. When Billy and Claire pay the company a visit, they get a frosty reception(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Kate Bliss help the teams in Stamford, Lincolnshire, and Natasha Raskin Sharp visits Burghley House to look at The Hell Staircase, a fine Baroque mural(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Zara joins a dating agency and decides to embrace it warts and all. Elsewhere Sid's discovery leaves Imogen terrified for her future. Valerie finds herself trapped in a game of cat-and-mouse with a besotted farmer who recently lost his wife(n)

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Today's Head Hunters are first-timer and civil servant Roisin, three-time Head Hunter Callum, and customer advisor Dubem, who has been on every show this week(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Sonali Shah makes a beeline for the desirable town of Sevenoaks in Kent, to help buyers who are selling up their London flat and sinking their £900,000 budget into a new life(n)

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Former legal director John Owens and property manager Julie Petford face each other in a bid to make the most profit and be crowned the winner in Stratford-upon-Avon(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore scours the recycling centre in Witley for items she can breathe new life into, including a tumble dryer drum and a teak record cabinet(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley and Patrick Kielty present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: England cricketer Joe Denly, former striker Chris Sutton, Great Britain sprinter Bianca Williams and Warrington Wolves winger Josh Charnley join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tensions between Martin and Ben reach breaking point, and the two soon come to blows. But can Martin convince Ben to let Stacey come home? A raging Stuart confronts Kathy for what she did to Rainie, Kush supports Jean as she attends Daniel's memorial service and Jack questions Gray about a fight he was involved with at a nightclub. Iqra becomes fed up of Arshad praising Adam and drops a bombshell about him cheating on Honey with Habiba(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The three remaining celebrities take part in the 2019 final, in which they must cook and present a faultless three-course meal for judgement by John Torode and Gregg Wallace, in what is their last chance to prove they have what it takes to be crowned champion(n)

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Actor Martin Clunes hosts as the satirical quiz returns for its 58th series, with two panellists joining regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events(n)

programme.title: Scarborough
programme.desc: In the final episode, tensions run high at Geraldine's salon when plans for the renovation are discussed, and Karen, Lisa Marie, Mandy and all the regulars fear what is going to happen to their beloved salon. Meanwhile at Marion's, Mr Ferris has popped round with a freshly baked pie and a very unexpected proposal, while at The Good Ship, Jack is making the final preparations for the big charity night which includes a meat raffle and some very special guests. Mike has agreed to sing and is surprised when an admirer makes him an offer which could change his and Karen's lives forever(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Among the guests on the sofa are singer-songwriter Bruce Springsteen, who talks about his new documentary Western Stars, Oscar winner Robert De Niro, starring in Martin Scorsese's The Irishman, and actor and comedian Paul Rudd, who discusses his new TV series Living with Yourself. Music is supplied by James Blunt, who performs Cold, from his new album(n)

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: Haunted by memories of her dead best friend, dumped by her emotionally fragile boyfriend, and now desperately trying to sell her stolen goods, Fleabag attempts to rekindle romantic fires to distract her from the mayhem of her life. Comedy, written by and starring Phoebe Waller-Bridge(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: For the second task of the competition, the contestants are challenged to create and sell their own range of ice lollies, pitching one product to a corporate client, and selling another to the general public. In the kitchen, one team decides to opt for risky ingredients, while on the other side, a last-minute switch leads to a tough corporate meeting. After the aspiring apprentices discover who is victorious, one of the losers will be shown the door by Alan Sugar before facing that notorious taxi ride home(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Josh Niland and Ben Tish, and special guest Jason Donovan. Plus, archive clips featuring Rick Stein, Keith Floyd, and Nigel Slater(n)

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Colleagues from companies across Scotland compete against each other in a series of baking challenges, to be crowned the companies baking champion. This time, four colleagues from Moray at the Forres branch of Moray Coast Vets - Mark, Johanna, Anna and Kristin - take part(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the forthcoming Euro 2020 qualifiers and review last night's match between the Czech Republic and England(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin visit a flat with sea views in Margate in Kent, a bungalow in Whaley Thorns in Nottinghamshire and an end terrace in Birmingham(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion and Nick Hall help two best friends and a married couple find items to sell at auction in Hemswell, Lincolnshire, and Anita Manning learns about Operation Fido(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades saves a battered suitcase, a wingback chair and a Formica table top from the recycling centre, and tries to transform them with the help of Daniel Heath and Ray Clarke(n)

programme.title: Live Gymnastics
programme.desc: Matt Baker presents live coverage of the penultimate day from Hanns Martin Schleyer Halle in Stuttgart, Germany, featuring the women's vault discipline. America's Simone Biles won four golds at last year's event, and will expect to be in contention for another title this afternoon. With analysis from Beth Tweddle, and commentary by Christine Still and Craig Heap(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor helps a couple search for a new home in South Wales, big enough to house their huge collection of superhero memorabilia(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys ushers comedian Joe Pasquale, Newsround presenter Ricky Boleto, singer Toyah Willcox and Olympic sprinter Jeanette Kwakye to the black chair to be put to the test on their chosen specialist subjects, which are werewolf films, number one singles of the 1990s, Boudica, and Disney animation respectively. The second round sees them tackling general knowledge questions in a bid to carry off the commemorative trophy(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman invite another selection of famous faces to take part in the unorthodox general knowledge quiz. Those standing behind the podiums this week are actors Michael Sheen, Steve Pemberton and Mark Benton, impressionist Jess Robinson, entertainers Anita Harris and Bobby Crush and former footballers Neil Ruddock and Steve McMahon(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present the fourth live episode of this year's series. Now that Anneka Rice has departed from the dancefloor, the 13 remaining couples hope to impress the judges and the viewers at home with an array of ballroom and Latin routines. Judges Motsi Mabuse, Craig Revel Horwood, Shirley Ballas and Bruno Tonioli will score the performances out of ten before the vote lines open - which gives viewers at home the chance to have their say and determine who will be leaving the competition(n)

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the first episode, sisters Louise and Helen from Leeds face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Despite receiving some devastating test results, Duffy continues to offer her support to others, which results in David and Dylan offering her a voluntary position in the emergency department. Meanwhile, Ethan struggles to keep his personal life from distracting him at work, and Archie turns to Will for help(n)

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes is disappointed to discover she is not invited to Maria's hen party because of her age, and decides to gatecrash the event, only to end up at the wrong venue. She then tries to impress the bride-to-be's mother during a dinner date, but is so nervous she turns to drink, and the evening descends into chaos. Comedy, written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of New England Patriots v New York Giants. Plus, a look at the major stories shaping the NFL world(n)

programme.title: The Client (1994)
programme.desc: A Mafia lawyer tells a boy where a politician's body is buried shortly before committing suicide, prompting a team of district attorneys to press the young witness for information. However, the streetwise kid refuses to give evidence unless his own and his family's safety is guaranteed, and hires a lawyer to ensure his demands are met. Courtroom thriller based on the novel by John Grisham, with Susan Sarandon, Tommy Lee Jones and Brad Renfro(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: At Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey, Sarah Moore rescues a vintage pram in the hope of generating a profit for its owners. A windbreaker and an old ottoman are also spruced up(n)

programme.title: Fugitives
programme.desc: Met Police officers head to Liverpool to arrest a man wanted in Bermuda, while in Derbyshire, detectives are looking for a Latvian man with a long history of crime(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce heads to the waterfront beside the V&A Dundee design museum, on the east coast of Scotland. She explores the fascinating story behind the landmark museum and the treasures it houses. Among the discoveries are a valuable bowl found at a car boot sale for just £3, a set of rare SAS medals and a Vivienne Westwood jacket(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Hardball
programme.desc: Contestants answer questions to get as far as they can along a track before a ball catches and passes them, ending their go. Quiz, hosted by Ore Oduba(n)

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: In this episode, Alice Roberts joins archaeologists in the North as they uncover writing tablets, swords and domestic items left by Romans at Vindolanda during a time of British rebellion, before finding traces of a long lost monastery and pilgrimage site on the Scottish island of Iona. Meanwhile in the east of Scotland, a weapons hoard belonging to a wealthy Bronze Age warrior is unearthed(n)

programme.title: Live Gymnastics
programme.desc: Matt Baker presents live coverage of the women's all-around final from Hanns Martin Schleyer Halle in Stuttgart, Germany. All eyes will be on American superstar Simone Biles, who is looking to extend her record after becoming the first woman to win this event four times when returning last year following a lengthy hiatus from competition. With analysis from Beth Tweddle, and commentary by Christine Still and Craig Heap(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Ade Edmondson, Ed Gamble, Miquita Oliver and Kate Thornton test their knowledge in a variety of trivia-based games across the week, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball dishes the backstage gossip, talks to some of the couples and has a sneak peek at a few of Saturday's costumes with designer Vicky Gill.(n)

programme.title: MOTDx
programme.desc: Jermaine Jenas is joined by Craig Mitch, Chelcee Grimes and Reece Parkinson to take an offbeat look at the Premier League, also discussing skills, celebrities, fashion, music and culture(n)

programme.title: The Channel Tunnel - Life on the Inside
programme.desc: With up to 400 trains a day running on its 62 miles of track, the Channel Tunnel is the busiest railway system in the world. And doing things at the double is key to its success. Two countries built it. There are two terminals. And there is duplication at both ends. This episode reveals how teams of two work on both sides of the Channel to keep tourists and truckers moving. But how will preparations for Brexit affect such a well-oiled system of twinning and duplication, finely tuned over 25 years?(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers: Route 66
programme.desc: For the fourth leg of their journey, Dave Myers and Si King travels the 400 miles from Amarillo, Texas to Albuquerque in New Mexico. In Amarillo, the pair visit the oldest burger joint in the city, before riding to Palo Duro Canyon to explore a ghost town. Finally arriving in Albuquerque, they join residents to watch a game at the local ballpark and bake a chili chocolate pie to celebrate their cross-country adventure(n)

programme.title: Million Dollar Wedding Planner
programme.desc: The exclusive world of Lelian Chew, who organises nuptials for the super-rich in the Far East, taking on 10 just ceremonies a year at an average cost of $1million each. Bespoke Cartier jewellery, hundreds of thousands of dollars of flowers and multiple designer wedding dresses are nothing out of the ordinary. But Lelian's job also involves navigating the clash between age-old Chinese marriage customs and the desires of the young, often with outlandish solutions - multiple weddings are becoming the norm for the Chinese super-rich(n)

programme.title: The Mash Report
programme.desc: Nish Kumar and a cast of comedy correspondents including Ellie Taylor and Steve N Allen present the satirical and surreal take on the week's news(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emma Barnett(n)

programme.title: Catching Britain's Killers: The Crimes That Changed Us
programme.desc: The stories of murder investigations and their consequences, which overturned laws, transformed police interrogation and revolutionised forensic detection. It begins with the hunt to find the killer of two teenage girls in 1980s Leicestershire, which led to an extraordinary forensic breakthrough - DNA fingerprinting(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, former The Only Way is Essex star and presenter Mark Wright investigates his family history. First, he examines his father's side of the family, and uncovers surprising facts about his Sephardic Jewish ancestry and his relation to a master swordsman living in late 17th-century Andalucia. Mark's journey then takes him to Spain, where he is astonished to find out how one of his relatives survived the terror of the Spanish Inquisition, and another was burned at the stake(n)

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges three artists in Birmingham to create a piece for a mystery buyer to reflect a family bond after donating half of her liver to her mother(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades rescues more items from the tip, finding a discarded pub table, a badminton supplies box and a vintage table which he and two other experts are charged with restyling(n)

programme.title: Fugitives
programme.desc: In London, police look for a man convicted of killing three police officers during the Balkan conflict. North Yorkshire officers arrest a man wanted for fraud and money laundering(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: An extended edition sees Monty Don add autumn perennials to his borders, show how to take root cuttings and reveal plans for next year when he plants bulbs for spring colour. On her Kent allotment, Frances Tophill catches up on some seasonal sowing and invites a special guest to help her with a small building project. Nick Bailey visits Cambridge University Botanic Garden, where he finds out how and why some plants produce their scent at the end of the day. The programme also returns to the wildlife garden of Kate Bradbury to find out about the lives of solitary bees(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Hardball
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Ore Oduba in which contestants have to answer questions to get as far along a track as they can before a ball catches and passes them, ending their go(n)

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: In a special episode, Alice Roberts sets off across Hadrian's Wall to reveal the forgotten story of the Roman Army's secret weapon in Britain - their cavalry. The programme also follows team of archaeologists and historical re-enactors as they attempt to re-stage a Roman cavalry tournament for a public display in Carlisle(n)

programme.title: Live Gymnastics
programme.desc: Matt Baker presents live coverage of the men's all-around final from Hanns Martin Schleyer Halle in Stuttgart, Germany. Russia's Artur Dalaloyan edged out defending champion Xiao Ruoteng in a tie-breaker in last year's event. Both will expect to be in contention once again, while the British duo of James Hall and Joe Fraser will also hope to have reached the final. With analysis from Beth Tweddle, and commentary by Christine Still and Craig Heap(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball and Rylan Clark-Neal present analysis of the pro-celebrity couples' progress ahead of tomorrow night's show, and reports from the rehearsals. Celebrity super fans discuss all things ballroom, while Gethin Jones catches up with the couples backstage at this week's Strictly rehearsals(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Ade Edmondson, Ed Gamble, Miquita Oliver and Kate Thornton go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games, vying to be crowned weekly champion(n)

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys asks questions on specialist subjects Birmingham City FC, Eurovision in the 21st Century, Plantagenet Queens and Members of the Weasel Family(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don gives tips on moving plants which are in the wrong place or have outgrown their space, shows how to store chillies and harvests the last of his tomatoes. Rachel de Thame heads to Longmeadow to talk to Monty about her return to health and to plant up pots for spring colour, while Nick Bailey travels to the Highlands in Scotland to find out about a project which is contributing to worldwide plant conservation(n)

programme.title: The Name of the Rose
programme.desc: In 1327, an enlightened Franciscan friar, William of Baskerville, and his young apprentice investigate a series of mysterious deaths at an abbey, risking the wrath of a powerful Inquisitor who has been sent by the Pope himself. John Turturro and Rupert Everett star in the latest adaptation of Umberto Eco's religious thriller, along with Damian Hardung, Michael Emerson and James Cosmo(n)

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti explore one of the world's most luxurious eco resorts on the small island of Tetiaroa in French Polynesia(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Alice Levine, Alan Carr and Phill Jupitus join Alan Davies in answering questions from Sandi Toksvig on the subject of Quests(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Mark Urban(n)

programme.title: Front Row Late
programme.desc: Mary Beard and guests are in Belfast, discussing the complex relationship between adults and young people. Plus, Mary talks to Charlotte Church about her experiences growing up(n)

programme.title: La La Land (2016)
programme.desc: Jobbing actress Mia and struggling jazz pianist Sebastian fall in love and attempt to realise their respective dreams in Los Angeles - a city known for crushing hopes and breaking hearts. However, keeping the wolf from the door without compromising their beliefs proves harder than expected. Damien Chazelle's Bafta-winning musical, starring Emma Stone, Ryan Gosling and John Legend(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Myles Bonnar investigates the multimillion-pound industry that sells men pick-up techniques to get women into bed as quickly as possible. He goes undercover as a student in one such group, and discovers an emphasis on getting from first encounter to sex as quickly as possible that crosses lines regarding consent(n)

programme.title: Gareth Thomas v Homophobia: The Legacy
programme.desc: The former rugby international continues his campaign to wipe out homophobia in football. With reported incidents of homophobic abuse at football matches at a record high, Gareth enlists the help of Parliament as he attempts to outlaw abuse in stadia. He also recruits Cardiff City, Leeds United and Norwich City in his campaign to tackle homophobia in the boardroom, the dressing room and on the terraces. All of this while coping with being the victim of a violent homophobic attack himself(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Ali-A's Superchargers
programme.desc: A sporty brother and sister from Wales modify their family car(n)

programme.title: Top Class
programme.desc: Two teams take on the challenge of answering questions(n)

programme.title: Mustangs FC
programme.desc: Liv questions whether soccer is really her passion(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Long-running children's magazine programme(n)

programme.title: Turbo (2013)
programme.desc: A garden snail obsessed with motor racing gets sucked into a car's exhaust pipe, and emerges having gained super-speed. This stroke of luck allows him to realise his dream of competing against human drivers in the prestigious Indianapolis 500 race. Animated adventure, with the voices of Ryan Reynolds, Paul Giamatti and Samuel L Jackson(n)

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and Angela Scanlon present another round of battles in the arena. An untested robot built by a cardiologist and his two sons makes a surprising impact in the arena, while a builder from Northampton wheels out an impressive looking axe-wielding robot. Plus, judge Sethu Vijayakumar discusses his work with Nasa, designing robots to explore Mars(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Tennis coach Judy Murray and former British number one Greg Rusedski compete, with expert guidance coming from Angus Ashworth and Raj Bisram(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers: Route 66
programme.desc: For the fourth leg of their journey, Dave Myers and Si King travels the 400 miles from Amarillo, Texas to Albuquerque in New Mexico. In Amarillo, the pair visit the oldest burger joint in the city, before riding to Palo Duro Canyon to explore a ghost town. Finally arriving in Albuquerque, they join residents to watch a game at the local ballpark and bake a chili chocolate pie to celebrate their cross-country adventure(n)

programme.title: The Best of British Takeaways
programme.desc: Chef Tom Kerridge tries to find out what makes the best Indian curry and uncovers the trade secrets behind our takeaways, while Cherry Healey explores the fascinating stories that helped shape our communities as well as our palates, and learns how Queen Victoria put curry on the British menu. Three award-winning Indian takeaways are then invited to compete in a contest in Bradford, each with their own ideas as to what makes the best takeaway, with Enam Ali acting as judge to decide the winner(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Three much-loved detached properties in Cardiff compete to be chosen for the final - a modern house with sea views, a country cottage, and a renovated farmhouse(n)

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Helen and Rob live in a Victorian terraced house in St Albans. They have put all their energy into renovating the entrance and the front room, but the back of the house remains a mishmash of tiny and unpractical rooms. Architects Robert Jamison and Laura Jane Clark compete to take control of this renovation project. Angela Scanlon presents(n)

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: James looks at some of the advances in motoring that never caught on, revealing how the petrol engine came to dominate in the 20th century, while concepts for jet engines and steam power fell by the wayside. He also drives an amphibious car and an electric scooter, and takes a ride in the world's fastest driverless car(n)

programme.title: Live Gymnastics
programme.desc: Matt Baker presents further live coverage of the penultimate day from Hanns Martin Schleyer Halle in Stuttgart, Germany, featuring the men's pommel horse. Great Britain's Max Whitlock is the reigning Olympic champion in this discipline, but was narrowly beaten into silver by China's Xiao Ruoteng in this event last year. With analysis from Beth Tweddle, and commentary by Christine Still and Craig Heap(n)

programme.title: River Walks
programme.desc: Former Coronation Street star Shobna Gulati takes a stroll through Shropshire and Worcestershire along the banks of the Severn, Great Britain's longest river, finding out about a part of the country that is a world away from her native north-west England(n)

programme.title: What Britain Buys and Sells in a Day
programme.desc: The team looks at the seafood trade, with Ed Balls finding out how the cargo hub at Heathrow is a crucial link in Britain's supply chain and going behind the scenes of the airport operations centre to witness how delays can affect it. Cherry Healey is in the Highlands to find out why the world is so fond of salmon farmed there and fishes for langoustine on Loch Fyne, while Ade Adepitan travels to Iceland to catch one of Britain's favourite type of fish and unravels the story of the Cod Wars(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Captain Mainwaring camps the platoon in a deserted barn, unaware it is inside the target range of an artillery exercise. Only Corporal Jones knows the danger, leading to a desperate race to raise the alarm in time. Clive Dunn, Arthur Lowe and John Le Mesurier star in an episode first shown in November 1972(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan comperes an evening of stand-up at London's Hammersmith Apollo, treating the audience to his own brand of humour before introducing routines by comedy circuit stars Jason Byrne and Stewart Francis(n)

programme.title: Mark Ronson: From the Heart
programme.desc: The songwriter and producer discusses his life and musical influences, including how the break-up of his marriage has influenced his most recent album, Late Night Feelings. The programme follows Ronson in London, Los Angeles and New York at work and at home, and there are interviews with family and friends, along with the likes of Lady Gaga, Simon Le Bon, Josh Homme and Bradley Cooper(n)

programme.title: Tron: Legacy (2010)
programme.desc: Ground-breaking computer genius Kevin Flynn disappears without trace. Years later, his son Sam goes looking for him, and is transported into a computer-generated world where he is forced to compete in deadly gladiatorial combat by an evil duplicate of his father plotting to wage war on the real world. Sci-fi adventure, with Jeff Bridges and Garrett Hedlund(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Handmade in Bolton
programme.desc: Shaun Greenhalgh is set the task of making a Renaissance animal plate of the type invented by Bernard Palissy. The 16th-century French potter used live reptiles and other animals on his ceramic ware, but for Shaun, killing grass snakes in Lancashire is not an option. So how can he source some examples for his moulds? Presented by Janina Ramirez(n)

programme.title: Forces of Nature with Brian Cox
programme.desc: The physicist goes on a grand tour of the planet to explain how the Earth's beauty is created by just a handful of forces, beginning by examining the diversity of shapes in the natural world. Off the coast of Canada, Brian explains how massive icebergs that surge down from Greenland and into shipping lanes of the Atlantic emerge due to a powerful yet infinitesimal force of nature, while revealing how honeycombs made by bees in Nepal to store their precious honey conceal a mathematical rule(n)

programme.title: Eugenics: Science's Greatest Scandal
programme.desc: Concluding the two-part documentary exploring the shocking story of eugenics, the controversial idea that the human race can be improved by selective breeding. It is commonly thought that the idea disappeared after the horrors of the Nazi Holocaust, but as science journalist Angela Saini and disability rights activist Adam Pearson discover, practices such as the sterilisation of the poor continued on a global scale for many more years(n)

programme.title: World War Two: 1945 and the Wheelchair President
programme.desc: David Reynolds examines the wartime leadership of US president Franklin Roosevelt, who was burdened by secrets about his failing health and strained marriage(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Michael Mosley explores the latest research in genetics and neuroscience to find out what factors shape people's personalities and whether they can be changed. Michael tries two techniques in an attempt to make him worry less and become more of an optimist - with surprising results(n)

programme.title: Handmade in Bolton
programme.desc: Dr Janina Ramirez sets ex-forger Shaun Greenhalgh the challenge of making an alabaster carving of the kind mass-produced in Catholic England in the centuries before the Reformation(n)

programme.title: Handmade in Bolton
programme.desc: Shaun Greenhalgh is set the task of making a Renaissance animal plate of the type invented by Bernard Palissy. The 16th-century French potter used live reptiles and other animals on his ceramic ware, but for Shaun, killing grass snakes in Lancashire is not an option. So how can he source some examples for his moulds? Presented by Janina Ramirez(n)

programme.title: Forces of Nature with Brian Cox
programme.desc: The physicist goes on a grand tour of the planet to explain how the Earth's beauty is created by just a handful of forces, beginning by examining the diversity of shapes in the natural world. Off the coast of Canada, Brian explains how massive icebergs that surge down from Greenland and into shipping lanes of the Atlantic emerge due to a powerful yet infinitesimal force of nature, while revealing how honeycombs made by bees in Nepal to store their precious honey conceal a mathematical rule(n)

programme.title: Eugenics: Science's Greatest Scandal
programme.desc: Concluding the two-part documentary exploring the shocking story of eugenics, the controversial idea that the human race can be improved by selective breeding. It is commonly thought that the idea disappeared after the horrors of the Nazi Holocaust, but as science journalist Angela Saini and disability rights activist Adam Pearson discover, practices such as the sterilisation of the poor continued on a global scale for many more years(n)

programme.title: World News Today
programme.desc: The day's leading stories(n)

programme.title: The Live Lounge Show
programme.desc: Clara Amfo peeks behind the scenes of Radio 1's Live Lounge, featuring music by Lana Del Rey, Jax Jones and Sam Fender(n)

programme.title: Rock 'n' Roll America
programme.desc: Documentary examining the most important era in the history of popular music - the birth of rock 'n' roll. The opening programme focuses on the origins of the sound in 1950s America when the rhythm-driven mix of blues, boogie woogie and vocal harmony was nurtured by small independent record labels and championed by young music pioneers such as Fats Domino and Little Richard. This episode also looks at the start of Elvis Presley's career in Memphis and examines the impact the film industry had on the rock movement. Contributors include Tom Jones, Jerry Lee Lewis and Don Everly(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Janice Long and Mark Goodier present the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 4 August 1988. Featuring Kim Wilde, The Funky Worm and Fairground Attraction(n)

programme.title: Studio 17: The Lost Reggae Tapes
programme.desc: A treasure trove of tapes from the golden age of reggae provides the soundtrack to the compelling story of the family behind Randy's Studio 17, established above Randy's Records at 17 North Parade in downtown Kingston, Jamaica. With contributions by Jimmy Cliff, Carl Malcolm, Ali Campbell, Bunny Lee, King Jammy, Rico Rodriguez, Clive Chin and Maxi Priest(n)

programme.title: Classic Albums
programme.desc: Concert footage, home movies and interviews in Jamaica give a background to the Wailers' LP Catch a Fire, which launched Bob Marley onto the global music scene. Record label boss Chris Blackwell reveals how he masterminded the overdubbing of the album for the rock market and Rabbit Brundrick (keyboards) and Wayne Perkins (electric guitar) tell how they were brought back in to add their parts to the songs(n)

programme.title: Reggae Fever: David Rodigan
programme.desc: The life of the German-born reggae broadcaster and radio presenter whose love affair with Jamaican music began in the 1950s, celebrating a man whose career is strangely intertwined with not only the evolution of such music in this country, but also the evolution of the culture. In recent years, the 67-year-old's DJ appearances have started attracting a far younger audience, a reflection perhaps of the way different forms of music from the different cultures that have arrived in Britain over the last 70 years have integrated, taken root and spawned new scenes, attitudes and tastes(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Janice Long and Mark Goodier present the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 4 August 1988. Featuring Kim Wilde, The Funky Worm and Fairground Attraction(n)

programme.title: Rock 'n' Roll America
programme.desc: Documentary examining the most important era in the history of popular music - the birth of rock 'n' roll. The opening programme focuses on the origins of the sound in 1950s America when the rhythm-driven mix of blues, boogie woogie and vocal harmony was nurtured by small independent record labels and championed by young music pioneers such as Fats Domino and Little Richard. This episode also looks at the start of Elvis Presley's career in Memphis and examines the impact the film industry had on the rock movement. programme.title: How to Make a Number One Record
programme.desc: Documentary studying what it takes to propel a single to number one. Featuring a look at those who have succeeded in topping the charts, and the effect it had on their lives, alongside an examination of the roles played by art, science, chance and manipulation in reaching the pinnacle of pop(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Bill Bailey narrates this natural history documentary telling the story of some of nature's most extraordinary partnerships, revealing what sometimes happens when animals need an itch scratching, help getting into food or home security assistance. The answer is, to find a partner - and not necessarily one of the same species. Highlights include hippos joining forces with fish for a full-body exfoliation and ravens inviting wolverines over for some bone-crunching assistance with their dinner(n)

programme.title: Wild China
programme.desc: The documentary series exploring China's wildlife continues with a journey to the Yunnan Province, where parallel mountain ranges channel mighty rivers from Tibet southward to the tropics, and the landscape provides a home to diverse species. While the mountain forests are home to red pandas, the valleys below contain jungles inhabited by gibbons, sunbirds and elephants(n)

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: Commissioner Herville is found dead in a Chinese restaurant in the 13th arrondissement of Paris, and Gilou must break the news to Laure, who is being treated in a police rehabilitation centre. Gilou, now the head of the unit, must investigate with a new partner, fresh out of training. programme.desc: The making of Back to Black, the second and final studio album by singer-songwriter Amy Winehouse, which was released in October 2006 to wide critical and commercial acclaim. programme.title: The Met: Policing London
programme.desc: In 2018 the highest number of under 25s were killed in London in more a decade. This documentary follows the investigation into two of these murders in the borough of Haringey. DCI Luke Marks picks up the case of 22-year-old youth worker Kobi Nelson, who was stabbed near his home and died at the scene. Less than five weeks later, 19-year-old Kelvin Odunuyi is shot dead in Wood Green. Kelvin was a good student although his mother believes he was being groomed by local gangs. Was he killed in direct retaliation for Kobi's murder? It's up to Luke Marks and his team to find out(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Beckenham in Bromley, Greater London, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Former legal director John Owens and property manager Julie Petford face each other in a bid to make the most profit and be crowned the winner in Stratford-upon-Avon(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley and Patrick Kielty present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: Iolo's Street Life
programme.desc: In Iolo Williams' last urban wildlife tour, as the days get shorter, autumn and winter beckon. On a night hunt in Caernarfon he finds spiders that can give a good bite. Stunning birds from Scandinavia drop in at a retail park in Llansamlet, while on an ordinary housing estate in Llangefni on Anglesey, rare red squirrels visit for their winter supply of nuts(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tensions between Martin and Ben reach breaking point, and the two soon come to blows. But can Martin convince Ben to let Stacey come home? A raging Stuart confronts Kathy for what she did to Rainie, Kush supports Jean as she attends Daniel's memorial service and Jack questions Gray about a fight he was involved with at a nightclub. Iqra becomes fed up of Arshad praising Adam and drops a bombshell about him cheating on Honey with Habiba(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The three remaining celebrities take part in the 2019 final, in which they must cook and present a faultless three-course meal for judgement by John Torode and Gregg Wallace, in what is their last chance to prove they have what it takes to be crowned champion(n)

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Actor Martin Clunes hosts as the satirical quiz returns for its 58th series, with panellists Layla Moran MP and Sara Pascoe joining regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events(n)

programme.title: Scarborough
programme.desc: In the final episode, tensions run high at Geraldine's salon when plans for the renovation are discussed, and Karen, Lisa Marie, Mandy and all the regulars fear what is going to happen to their beloved salon. Meanwhile at Marion's, Mr Ferris has popped round with a freshly baked pie and a very unexpected proposal, while at The Good Ship, Jack is making the final preparations for the big charity night which includes a meat raffle and some very special guests. Mike has agreed to sing and is surprised when an admirer makes him an offer which could change his and Karen's lives forever(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Among the guests on the sofa are singer-songwriter Bruce Springsteen, who talks about his new documentary Western Stars, Oscar winner Robert De Niro, starring in Martin Scorsese's The Irishman, and actor and comedian Paul Rudd, who discusses his new TV series Living with Yourself. Music is supplied by James Blunt, who performs Cold, from his new album(n)

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: Haunted by memories of her dead best friend, dumped by her emotionally fragile boyfriend, and now desperately trying to sell her stolen goods, Fleabag attempts to rekindle romantic fires to distract her from the mayhem of her life. Comedy, written by and starring Phoebe Waller-Bridge(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: For the second task of the competition, the contestants are challenged to create and sell their own range of ice lollies, pitching one product to a corporate client, and selling another to the general public. In the kitchen, one team decides to opt for risky ingredients, while on the other side, a last-minute switch leads to a tough corporate meeting. After the aspiring apprentices discover who is victorious, one of the losers will be shown the door by Alan Sugar before facing that notorious taxi ride home(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Josh Niland and Ben Tish, and special guest Jason Donovan. Plus, archive clips featuring Rick Stein, Keith Floyd, and Nigel Slater(n)

programme.title: Down the Social with Owen Money
programme.desc: Mandy Starr and Owen visit the Newbridge Memo, exploring the 1920s art deco theatre and the ballroom where young locals in the 1940s and 50s would come to see dance bands. They talk to a couple who met at the venue half a century ago and still attend dances there today(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the forthcoming Euro 2020 qualifiers and review last night's match between the Czech Republic and England(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin visit a flat with sea views in Margate in Kent, a bungalow in Whaley Thorns in Nottinghamshire and an end terrace in Birmingham(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion and Nick Hall help two best friends and a married couple find items to sell at auction in Hemswell, Lincolnshire, and Anita Manning learns about Operation Fido(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades saves a battered suitcase, a wingback chair and a Formica table top from the recycling centre, and tries to transform them with the help of Daniel Heath and Ray Clarke(n)

programme.title: Live Gymnastics
programme.desc: Matt Baker presents live coverage of the penultimate day from Hanns Martin Schleyer Halle in Stuttgart, Germany, featuring the women's vault discipline. America's Simone Biles won four golds at last year's event, and will expect to be in contention for another title this afternoon. With analysis from Beth Tweddle, and commentary by Christine Still and Craig Heap(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys ushers comedian Joe Pasquale, Newsround presenter Ricky Boleto, singer Toyah Willcox and Olympic sprinter Jeanette Kwakye to the black chair to be put to the test on their chosen specialist subjects, which are werewolf films, number one singles of the 1990s, Boudica, and Disney animation respectively. The second round sees them tackling general knowledge questions in a bid to carry off the commemorative trophy(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: England cricketer Joe Denly, former striker Chris Sutton, Great Britain sprinter Bianca Williams and Warrington Wolves winger Josh Charnley join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman invite another selection of famous faces to take part in the unorthodox general knowledge quiz. Those standing behind the podiums this week are actors Michael Sheen, Steve Pemberton and Mark Benton, impressionist Jess Robinson, entertainers Anita Harris and Bobby Crush and former footballers Neil Ruddock and Steve McMahon(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present the fourth live episode of this year's series. Now that Anneka Rice has departed from the dancefloor, the 13 remaining couples hope to impress the judges and the viewers at home with an array of ballroom and Latin routines. Judges Motsi Mabuse, Craig Revel Horwood, Shirley Ballas and Bruno Tonioli will score the performances out of ten before the vote lines open - which gives viewers at home the chance to have their say and determine who will be leaving the competition(n)

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the first episode, sisters Louise and Helen from Leeds face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Despite receiving some devastating test results, Duffy continues to offer her support to others, which results in David and Dylan offering her a voluntary position in the emergency department. Meanwhile, Ethan struggles to keep his personal life from distracting him at work, and Archie turns to Will for help(n)

programme.title: Gareth Edwards: Rugby, Apartheid and Me
programme.desc: Fifty years ago, Gareth Edwards captained Wales during a Springbok's tour disrupted by anti-apartheid protests. Now, Gareth meets players and protesters from the controversial rugby tours of 1969 and 1974 and discovers how these events changed South Africa. Later, Gareth travels to see a game in a township Port Elizabeth and witnesses for himself the present day cost of apartheid for millions of people in South Africa(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of New England Patriots v New York Giants. Plus, a look at the major stories shaping the NFL world(n)

programme.title: The Met: Policing London
programme.desc: In 2018 the highest number of under 25s were killed in London in more a decade. This documentary follows the investigation into two of these murders in the borough of Haringey. DCI Luke Marks picks up the case of 22-year-old youth worker Kobi Nelson, who was stabbed near his home and died at the scene. Less than five weeks later, 19-year-old Kelvin Odunuyi is shot dead in Wood Green. Kelvin was a good student although his mother believes he was being groomed by local gangs. Was he killed in direct retaliation for Kobi's murder? It's up to Luke Marks and his team to find out(n)

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: Record numbers of people are now being treated for a whole range of problems from anxiety and depression to critical illnesses like dementia. Mental health problems are as common in older adults as younger ones, yet it is claimed people are less likely to get help as they age. Fiona Foster investigates Britain's mental health crisis(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Robert and Victoria prepare for the future, Mandy capitalises on an opportunity, and Charity continues to tease Tracy(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady's Little Heroes
programme.desc: Paul meets 13-year-old Noah, who has checked into the neurology ward ahead of an operation to correct a structural defect of the skull. The youngster needs to overcome his anxiety, however, so Paul joins him on a fun tour aimed at demystifying surgical theatres. Other children include seven-year-old Oliver, a long-term patient who is struggling to walk after being struck down by arthritis, and 17-year-old Rue, who has lost much of her sight and is now using various gadgets to re-learn many everyday tasks(n)

programme.title: Inside Prison: Britain Behind Bars
programme.desc: The documentary lifting the lid on life behind bars focuses on HMP Isle of Wight, a specialist prison housing many sex offenders and paedophiles. Intelligence has been gathered that a USB stick containing child pornography is doing the rounds, so the officers launch a search. But finding such a small item in a vast prison is no easy task. The staff also face a constant battle against drugs, with the inmates finding increasingly sophisticated and creative ways to smuggle them in, from letters sprayed with a spice solution to hollowed-out chess pieces(n)

programme.title: Sanditon
programme.desc: As the Sanditon summer regatta approaches, Sidney wrestles with his feelings towards an old flame and Young Stringer reveals his feelings to Charlotte. With Lady Denham at death's door, the tensions between Edward, Clara and Esther finally come to a head - with surprising consequences. Penultimate episode of Andrew Davies's adaptation of the unfinished final novel by Jane Austen about a spirited young woman who finds herself in a seaside resort on the cusp of dramatic change. Starring Rose Williams, Theo James and Anne Reid(n)

programme.title: Live International Football
programme.desc: Czech Republic v England (Kick-off 7.45pm). All the action from the Group A clash at Sinobo Stadium in Prague. A hat-trick by Raheem Sterling, a Harry Kane penalty and a Tomas Kalas own goal saw England get their qualifying campaign under way with an emphatic 5-0 triumph in March's reverse fixture, and they have maintained their 100 per cent record since then with victories over Montenegro, Bulgaria and Kosovo. However, the Czechs have won both of their home matches in the group, so will be expecting to provide a sterner test for Gareth Southgate's men on this occasion. With analysis from Roy Keane, Ian Wright and Lee Dixon, and commentary by Clive Tyldesley and Glenn Hoddle(n)

programme.title: International Football Highlights
programme.desc: Czech Republic v England. Action from the Group A qualifier at Sinobo Stadium in Prague. Gareth Southgate's visitors had been in dominant form in the group so far, and another victory would have seen them guarantee top spot with two games to spare, but there were signs of defensive vulnerability in their 5-3 victory over Kosovo last time out at Southampton's St Mary's Stadium. With analysis from Glenn Hoddle and Lee Dixon(n)

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent: The Champions
programme.desc: After five weeks of previous champions from around the world taking to the stage, Ant and Dec introduce the final in which the overall winner is eventually revealed. Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon and David Walliams will be giving their verdict on the evening's acts(n)

programme.title: The Level
programme.desc: As police comb the haulage yard for clues, Nancy must hide her guilt from both a fraught Hayley and her colleagues, but realises she has left incriminating evidence at the scene. Newman mounts a risky armed operation to ensnare both Elliot and the truck, although events spiral out of control when Nancy goes unprotected into the lion's den - with unexpected and bloody consequences(n)

programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: RWC 2019 Live
programme.desc: New Zealand v Italy (Kick-off 5.45am). Martin Bayfield presents coverage of both teams' fourth and final Pool B match, held at City of Toyota Stadium. With analysis from Lawrence Dallaglio, Sean Fitzpatrick and Clive Woodward, and commentary by Martin Gillingham and Scott Hastings(n)

programme.title: RWC 2019 Live
programme.desc: England v France (Kick-off 9.15am). Mark Pougatch presents all the action from both teams' final match in Pool C, held at International Stadium Yokohama. France prevailed 19-12 in the 2011 quarter-finals when the sides last met in this competition, but England have won seven of the 10 contests between the teams since, including a 44-8 thrashing at Twickenham in this year's Six Nations. With analysis from Jonny Wilkinson, Clive Woodward and Lawrence Dallaglio, and commentary by Nick Mullins, Ugo Monye and Ben Kay(n)

programme.title: RWC 2019 Live
programme.desc: Ireland v Samoa (Kick-off 11.45am). Craig Doyle presents all the action from both teams' fourth and concluding Pool A fixture, held at Fukuoka Hakatanomori Stadium in Japan. Ireland entered the tournament as the favourites to top the pool, but will be wary of complacency after their shock defeat against the hosts. With analysis from Brian O'Driscoll, Paul O'Connell and Ian McGeechan, and commentary by Miles Harrison and Alan Quinlan(n)

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present coverage of Future Champions' Day at Newmarket, including the 3.30 Darley Dewhurst Stakes and 4.10 Dubai Cesarewitch Stakes, which follow races at 2.20 and 2.55. Plus, races at 2.40, 3.15 and 3.50 from York. With analysis from Jason Weaver and Johnny Murtagh, reports by Oli Bell and Matt Chapman, and commentary from Richard Hoiles(n)

programme.title: Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a celebrity edition of the game show, asking contestants Johnny Vegas, Shaun Wallace and Brooke Vincent to 'see it and say it' as they try to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. After the first elimination, the two remaining players battle to reach the Super Catchphrase round, where one of them could win £50,000 for their chosen charity(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as TV presenters Donna Air, Amanda Lamb, Sarah Greene and Dan Snow pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds for charity. programme.title: The X Factor: Celebrity
programme.desc: Dermot O'Leary hosts a star-studded version of the singing contest, with judges Simon Cowell, Nicole Scherzinger and Louis Walsh mentoring the famous faces. The series kicks off at Simon's home in Los Angeles, where the celebs are split into three categories - Overs, Unders and Groups. Representing the Overs are journalist Martin Bashir, talk show host Ricki Lake, Chaser Jenny 'The Vixen' Ryan and actress Victoria Ekanoye. Reality star Megan McKenna, model and actress Hayley Hasselhoff, soap star Jonny Labey, film star Olivia Olson and actor Kevin McHale are the Unders, while the groups consist of Love Island's Samira Mighty, Zara McDermott, Wes Nelson and Eyal Booker, sports stars Ben Foden, Thom Evans and Levi Davis, dancer Brendan Cole and actor Jeremy Edwards, CBBC presenters Max and Harvey, and influencers Sofia, Alondra, Laura, Wendii and Natalie(n)

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: The host is joined on the sofa by renowned chef Gordon Ramsay, Team GB sprinter Dina Asher-Smith and stand-up comedian Rob Beckett. Plus, multi-platinum-selling Irish group the Script perform live in the studio(n)

programme.title: A Confession
programme.desc: Karen takes her fight for justice all the way to Downing Street, but it proves fruitless - the chance of seeing her daughter's killer stand trial is seemingly impossible. Fulcher's scandalous actions cost him his position in the police and see him forced to leave the country, only able to find work as a security consultant in African hotspots. However, fresh hope arises when Wiltshire Police find a way to possibly bring Christopher Halliwell to court for Becky's murder - but the disgraced detective must return home to face the man who ruined his career(n)

Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: Record numbers of people are now being treated for a whole range of problems from anxiety and depression to critical illnesses like dementia. Mental health problems are as common in older adults as younger ones, yet it is claimed people are less likely to get help as they age. Fiona Foster investigates Britain's mental health crisis(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale

programme.title: Paul O'Grady's Little Heroes
programme.desc: Paul meets 13-year-old Noah, who has checked into the neurology ward ahead of an operation to correct a structural defect of the skull. The youngster needs to overcome his anxiety, however, so Paul joins him on a fun tour aimed at demystifying surgical theatres. Other children include seven-year-old Oliver, a long-term patient who is struggling to walk after being struck down by arthritis, and 17-year-old Rue, who has lost much of her sight and is now using various gadgets to re-learn many everyday tasks(n)

programme.title: Inside Prison: Britain Behind Bars
programme.desc: The documentary lifting the lid on life behind bars focuses on HMP Isle of Wight, a specialist prison housing many sex offenders and paedophiles. Intelligence has been gathered that a USB stick containing child pornography is doing the rounds, so the officers launch a search. But finding such a small item in a vast prison is no easy task. The staff also face a constant battle against drugs, with the inmates finding increasingly sophisticated and creative ways to smuggle them in, from letters sprayed with a spice solution to hollowed-out chess pieces(n)

programme.title: Sanditon
programme.desc: As the Sanditon summer regatta approaches, Sidney wrestles with his feelings towards an old flame and Young Stringer reveals his feelings to Charlotte. With Lady Denham at death's door, the tensions between Edward, Clara and Esther finally come to a head - with surprising consequences. Penultimate episode of Andrew Davies's adaptation of the unfinished final novel by Jane Austen about a spirited young woman who finds herself in a seaside resort on the cusp of dramatic change. Starring Rose Williams, Theo James and Anne Reid(n)

programme.title: Live International Football
programme.desc: Czech Republic v England (Kick-off 7.45pm). All the action from the Group A clash at Sinobo Stadium in Prague. A hat-trick by Raheem Sterling, a Harry Kane penalty and a Tomas Kalas own goal saw England get their qualifying campaign under way with an emphatic 5-0 triumph in March's reverse fixture, and they have maintained their 100 per cent record since then with victories over Montenegro, Bulgaria and Kosovo. However, the Czechs have won both of their home matches in the group, so will be expecting to provide a sterner test for Gareth Southgate's men on this occasion. With analysis from Roy Keane, Ian Wright and Lee Dixon, and commentary by Clive Tyldesley and Glenn Hoddle(n)

programme.title: International Football Highlights
programme.desc: Czech Republic v England. Action from the Group A qualifier at Sinobo Stadium in Prague. Gareth Southgate's visitors had been in dominant form in the group so far, and another victory would have seen them guarantee top spot with two games to spare, but there were signs of defensive vulnerability in their 5-3 victory over Kosovo last time out at Southampton's St Mary's Stadium. With analysis from Glenn Hoddle and Lee Dixon(n)

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent: The Champions
programme.desc: After five weeks of previous champions from around the world taking to the stage, Ant and Dec introduce the final in which the overall winner is eventually revealed. Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon and David Walliams will be giving their verdict on the evening's acts(n)

programme.title: The Level
programme.desc: As police comb the haulage yard for clues, Nancy must hide her guilt from both a fraught Hayley and her colleagues, but realises she has left incriminating evidence at the scene. Newman mounts a risky armed operation to ensnare both Elliot and the truck, although events spiral out of control when Nancy goes unprotected into the lion's den - with unexpected and bloody consequences(n)

programme.title: RWC 2019 Live
programme.desc: England v France (Kick-off 9.15am). Mark Pougatch presents all the action from both teams' final match in Pool C, held at International Stadium Yokohama. France prevailed 19-12 in the 2011 quarter-finals when the sides last met in this competition, but England have won seven of the 10 contests between the teams since, including a 44-8 thrashing at Twickenham in this year's Six Nations. With analysis from Jonny Wilkinson, Clive Woodward and Lawrence Dallaglio, and commentary by Nick Mullins, Ugo Monye and Ben Kay(n)

programme.title: RWC 2019 Live
programme.desc: Ireland v Samoa (Kick-off 11.45am). Craig Doyle presents all the action from both teams' fourth and concluding Pool A fixture, held at Fukuoka Hakatanomori Stadium in Japan. David Suchet stars, with Philip Jackson and Hugh Fraser(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective reopens an unsolved case when a skeleton is discovered beneath the burned-out remains of a Whitley Bay nightclub. She suspects the bones belong to a notorious criminal who was once a friend of her father and has been missing for more than 20 years. When another murder occurs nearby, she investigates the possibility that a local crime family may be involved with both deaths, while the original owners of the club also have secrets to hide. Brenda Blethyn stars in an adaptation of Ann Cleeves' 2017 Vera Stanhope novel The Seagull(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin investigate when former top-flight footballer Robbie Nichols is murdered in the East End of London, in what appears to be a robbery gone wrong. They question a man seen in the area on the night of the killing, who protests his innocence and points the finger at a known gangster he claims was also at the scene. With the detectives at odds over who to believe, Steel finds it increasingly difficult to prosecute the accused. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Ben Daniels star(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: High-court judge Rachel Callaghan (guest star Juliet Stevenson) is critically injured after being shot in the underground car park of her apartment. programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: An American female bishop visiting St Gerard's College is found dead after drinking poisoned wine, and when another two killings occur, both mirroring macabre murders from Jacobean revenge tragedy, it appears the culprit is targeting candidates in the election to become vice-regent. However, when Innocent learns one of the suspects is hiding a dark secret, Lewis and Hathaway realise the motive is much more twisted. Guest starring Sian Phillips and Ronald Pickup, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: A farmer's murder and the crash landing of an enemy plane lead Foyle to investigate the activities of a group of land girls, and the detective begins to suspect the captured German airmen have something to hide. Stella Gonet and James Wilby guest star in the wartime drama, with Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks(n)

programme.title: The Street
programme.desc: Drama by Jimmy McGovern set in the north of England, chronicling the eventful lives of a disparate community. Each episode focuses on the experiences of a different family on the street. A mother-of-three is frustrated with her mundane marriage, but an affair with a neighbour offers the excitement she's been missing. Starring Jane Horrocks, Jim Broadbent and Timothy Spall(n)

programme.title: The Street
programme.desc: A man dreading the prospect of retirement becomes even more depressed when his company allows him to leave his job early. Things only get worse when he discovers his pension will not nearly amount to enough for he and his wife to live on, but a strange turn of events next door brings a new meaning to his life. Starring Jim Broadbent and Sue Johnston(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An archaeologist dies of a heart attack shortly after smashing the seal of an ancient Egyptian tomb, raising the spectre of the mummy's curse. The grieving widow summons the Belgian sleuth to unravel the mystery, but he can only smoke out the real villain by risking his own life. David Suchet stars, with Anna Cropper and Bill Bailey(n)

programme.title: Wild at Heart
programme.desc: Danny finds a potential lifeline for the family as he pitches for a grant from a pharmaceutical company looking to fund an environmental project. The money would enable them to buy their former home, but Danny's old boss Ed Lynch turns out to be his rival. Du Plessis is determined to do whatever it takes to prevent the sale of Leopard's Den to Lynch - but his plan has disastrous consequences(n)

programme.title: Wild at Heart
programme.desc: Caroline is opposed to working with Ed Lynch and is furious at what she sees as Danny's betrayal of Du Plessis, whom she plans to reinstall in his rightful place in the business with the help of a familiar face from the past. However, when a bush fire spirals out of control, there are devastating consequences for the entire family. African-set wildlife drama, starring Hayley Mills, Stephen Tompkinson and Deon Stewardson(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan is offered a job in a car factory in the midlands and decides the best way to quit his job at the bus depot is by getting himself sacked in order to earn an extra week's wages. Reg Varney stars(n)

programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Jack and the inspector try to fix a water heater, but their efforts end in disaster. Comedy, starring Stephen Lewis and Bob Grant(n)

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: The hospital has its hands full when a typhoid epidemic breaks out in the community. Things get worse as the disease's source is traced back to the wards and staff struggle to locate it. Dr Weatherill is forced to reprimand Dr Ormerod after catching him sleeping on the job - but also has startling news for him. Drama, starring Robert Daws and Amy Robbins(n)

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Frankie accidentally injures a man attempting to steal his motorbike, but is stricken with guilt when the thief dies after crashing the vehicle. A woman being treated for severe burns disappears home, where Drs Ormerod and Weatherill discover her family secret, while Alun becomes obsessed by an expensive stereo system. With Scott Taylor, Robert Daws and Amy Robbins(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Troy is playing in an annual cricket match between Midsomer Worthy and Fletchers Cross when tragedy strikes. The wife of one of the team captains, a wealthy local landowner, is found battered to death with a cricket bat in a quarry. As Barnaby and Troy investigate, they discover no end of people with a grudge against the victim's husband - who may have been complicit in some other suspicious deaths. Guest starring Imelda Staunton and Robert Hardy(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby visits his Aunt Alice, who is recovering from an operation at a nursing home, and finds himself in the middle of a mystery. A resident has been found dead and the official verdict is a heart attack, but as rumours of fraud and deception abound, Alice's friend believes the matron and an obnoxious doctor may have conspired to commit murder. Whodunit, starring John Nettles, Daniel Casey, Jane Wymark and Laura Howard(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A petty thief is impaled with a pitchfork in sleepy Midsomer Mallow, jeopardising the residents' chances of winning a 'perfect village' competition. To make matters worse, two of the judges are also killed. Murder mystery, starring John Nettles, Daniel Casey, Timothy West and Maggie Steed, along with an appearance by Orlando Bloom(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: A farmer's murder and the crash landing of an enemy plane lead Foyle to investigate the activities of a group of land girls, and the detective begins to suspect the captured German airmen have something to hide. Stella Gonet and James Wilby guest star in the wartime drama, with Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: John Barnaby arrives in Midsomer and the headmistress of an upmarket girls' boarding school wastes no time in asking him to patrol a forthcoming classic-car show. However, when the local DJ who was set to judge the vehicles is crushed to death, the detective is soon investigating his first murder case since replacing cousin Tom - and as the death toll rises, he finds himself in a perilous situation. Drama, guest starring David Warner and Samantha Bond, with Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The detective drama prequel takes inspiration from an episode from Oxford's real-life history, in which Nation of Islam preacher and civil rights campaigner Marcus X was invited to speak at the Oxford Union in December 1964, less than two months before his assassination in New York. Here, Morse struggles to negotiate escalating social tensions as a protest at a hair salon exposes rapidly escalating racial unrest in the heart of the city. Meanwhile, a murder at an Army base threatens to unveil long-held secrets within the regiment. Guest starring Marcus Griffiths, with Shaun Evans and Roger Allam(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Newly promoted DS Hazel Wallace returns from training to investigate when a man is found hanged - a case further complicated by the fact that, according to police records, the victim actually died a year previously. It is not long before Jack is back from his extended compassionate leave to help her sort out the whole sorry mess. David Jason stars, with Caroline Harker, Kate McEnery and Cheryl Campbell(n)

programme.desc: The Sugdens move their furniture out of the farmhouse. Rachel is furious when she catches Jayesh reading her mail. Lynn is propositioned by Frank(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat

Drama, with Jason Durr making his final appearance as Mike Bradley(n)

programme.desc: Jack and Blakey turn out to be the only entrants in a gardening competition organised by the depot manager's wife. Seventies comedy, starring Bob Grant(n)

programme.desc: The detective reopens an unsolved case when a skeleton is discovered beneath the burned-out remains of a Whitley Bay nightclub. She suspects the bones belong to a notorious criminal who was once a friend of her father and has been missing for more than 20 years. When another murder occurs nearby, she investigates the possibility that a local crime family may be involved with both deaths, while the original owners of the club also have secrets to hide. Brenda Blethyn stars in an adaptation of Ann Cleeves' 2017 Vera Stanhope novel The Seagull(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin investigate when former top-flight footballer Robbie Nichols is murdered in the East End of London, in what appears to be a robbery gone wrong. With the detectives at odds over who to believe, Steel finds it increasingly difficult to prosecute the accused. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Ben Daniels star(n)
